name: config-hmcl
description: Raw-edits the native game launcher's own JSON config for launcher settings that have no dedicated tool action. Covers Java path, game directory, window size, theme/background, and proxy — but never memory, JVM/GC args, or options.txt (each has a dedicated instance tool). Use when the user changes a launcher-level setting ("换java路径","窗口大小","启动器背景","代理","游戏目录"); configuring the AI assistant itself is config-hmcl-ae.
body:
# Configuring native HMCL

HMCL stores settings as schema-versioned JSON files. For the settings this skill actually
covers below (the ones with no dedicated tool action), use `read`/`edit`/`write` directly on
the files — raw file editing, not the `instance` tool. Keep each file's `"$schema"` key intact
or HMCL discards the file as invalid.

## Do NOT raw-edit these three — dedicated tools exist

- Memory → `instance(action="set_memory", maxMemoryMB)`.
- JVM/GC args → `instance(action="set_jvm_args", jvmArgs)`.
- Game options (`options.txt`) → `instance(action="set_option", key, value)`.

These exist precisely so `instance-game-settings.json` is never hand-edited for them.
`HMCLGameRepository` caches every LOADED instance's game settings in memory and does a
full-file overwrite the next time ANY OTHER property on that same instance changes (memory,
isolation, a mod toggle, …). A raw file write never updates that in-memory copy, so a
hand-edited value for one of these three can be silently clobbered — with no error, no
warning — the very next time something unrelated changes for that instance. The dedicated
tool actions mutate the SAME in-memory object HMCL itself holds, so they don't have this
problem. If asked to change one of these three, call the tool action, report the
previous/new value it returns, and stop — do not also touch the JSON file "just in case".

## Critical: in-memory settings, not just exit-time rewrite

HMCL holds `launcher-settings.json` / `game-settings.json` in memory and **overwrites them on
exit** — edit those only while HMCL is closed (or via the UI), then tell the user to reopen it.
`instance-game-settings.json` is ALSO cached in memory for every loaded instance (see above),
so it is only safe to raw-edit for fields with no dedicated tool action (Java path, window
size, etc.) — and even then, tell the user a restart/relaunch of that instance is needed
before the change applies; do not assume editing it live is risk-free.

## File locations (`<.hmcl>` = `<launch dir>/.hmcl`, same relative layout on every OS since it's
resolved from wherever HMCL is launched from)

| Concern | File |
|---|---|
| Launcher (theme, proxy, language, background, download) | `<.hmcl>/config/launcher-settings.json` |
| User settings (Java list, log retention) — Windows | `%APPDATA%/.hmcl/config/user-settings.json` |
| User settings (Java list, log retention) — macOS | `~/Library/Application Support/hmcl/config/user-settings.json` |
| User settings (Java list, log retention) — Linux | `~/.local/share/hmcl/config/user-settings.json` (or `$XDG_DATA_HOME/hmcl/config/user-settings.json` if `XDG_DATA_HOME` is set) |
| Game directories / profiles | `<.hmcl>/config/game-directories.json` |
| Game settings presets ("global") | `<.hmcl>/config/game-settings.json` (`presets` array) |
| Per-instance overrides | `<gameDir>/versions/<ver>/.hmcl/config/instance-game-settings.json` |

The "User settings" file lives in HMCL's per-OS shared user-data home, not `<.hmcl>` — the other
rows are all under `<.hmcl>` (the launch-directory-relative folder) and are identical across
platforms. Memory (`maxMemory`) and JVM args (`jvmOptions`) live in the last two files too, but
are listed here for reading/reference only — never write them directly; use the tool actions
above.

## overrideProperties gotcha

`instance-game-settings.json` only applies a field if its name is ALSO listed in
`"overrideProperties"` — otherwise it silently inherits from `"parent"`. Set BOTH the value
AND add the key to `overrideProperties`, e.g. `"windowType":"FULLSCREEN"` alone does nothing.
To change a setting globally, edit the matching preset in `game-settings.json` instead (no
`overrideProperties` needed there).

## After any change

State which file(s) changed, whether it needs HMCL closed/reopened or just an instance
relaunch, and never fabricate account credentials or IDs.
